description: Fix resource leaks and cleanup failures in E2E tests where manually-managed test infrastructure crashes in the finally block when partial setup fails, leaving orphaned servers, temp dirs, and browser contexts behind.

userStories.title: Fix partial-setup resource leak in tab-state-sync.e2e.ts reconnect test
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the reconnect test's finally block to handle undefined variables gracefully so that server processes and temp directories are cleaned up even when launchExtensionContext fails.
acceptanceCriteria: The finally block at line ~372 uses optional chaining or conditional checks for context, cleanupDir, and extensionDir before calling .close() or fs.rmSync()
If launchExtensionContext throws, server1, testServer, and configDir are still properly cleaned up
The test still passes when all setup steps succeed (no behavioral regression)
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/tab-state-sync.e2e.ts, lines 270-377. The test creates resources sequentially: configDir (line 277), server1 (280), testServer (282), then destructures { context, cleanupDir, extensionDir } from launchExtensionContext (283). If launchExtensionContext throws, the const destructuring never assigns the variables. The finally block (lines 371-377) then crashes on `await context.close()` (undefined), `fs.rmSync(cleanupDir, ...)` (undefined), etc., masking the original error and leaving server1 and testServer running.

Fix pattern: declare context, cleanupDir as `let` variables before the try block, assign them inside try, and use `context?.close()` and conditional `if (cleanupDir) fs.rmSync(...)` in finally. The server and testServer kills should also use optional chaining. Model after the pattern already used in helpers.ts setupIsolatedIifeTest (lines 437-484) which tracks resources incrementally.

userStories.title: Fix partial-setup resource leaks in side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want all three manually-managed tests in side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts to handle partial setup failures gracefully so that orphaned processes and temp dirs are cleaned up.
acceptanceCriteria: The connection status test (line ~31) handles undefined context/cleanupDir in its finally block
The tab state dot test (line ~95) handles undefined context/cleanupDir in its finally block
The tool invocation animation test (line ~329) handles undefined context/cleanupDir in its finally block
All three tests still pass when setup succeeds (no behavioral regression)
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts. Three tests at lines ~31, ~95, ~329 all have the same pattern: they destructure { context, cleanupDir, extensionDir } from launchExtensionContext, and the finally blocks assume these variables are defined. If launchExtensionContext throws, the finally blocks crash.

Fix: Declare context, cleanupDir, extensionDir as let variables before the try block (initialized to undefined). Assign them inside try via: `const ext = await launchExtensionContext(...); context = ext.context; cleanupDir = ext.cleanupDir; extensionDir = ext.extensionDir;`. In finally, use `await context?.close()` and `if (cleanupDir) fs.rmSync(cleanupDir, ...)`. Same pattern in all three tests.

userStories.title: Fix tmpDir leak in strict-csp.e2e.ts multi-plugin and file-watcher tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want temporary directories created in strict-csp tests to be cleaned up in try/finally blocks so they don't leak when assertions fail.
acceptanceCriteria: The multi-plugin CSP tests (lines ~586, ~653, ~712) wrap their tmpDir cleanup in try/finally
tmpDir is cleaned up even when assertions or awaits throw before the cleanup line
All strict-csp tests still pass (no behavioral regression)

userStories.title: Fix analyze-site.e2e.ts silent failure cascade when beforeAll fails
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the analyze-site tests to fail with a clear error message when the shared test server fails to start, instead of producing 12 misleading test failures with empty URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: If startAnalyzeSiteServer() in beforeAll throws, subsequent tests fail with a clear message like 'Analyze-site test server failed to start' instead of obscure tool errors
The analyzeSiteServer?.url ?? '' fallback pattern is replaced with an explicit guard that throws early
All analyze-site tests still pass when the server starts successfully
